texto: Happiness is like time and space-we make and measure it ourselves it is as fancy as big as little as you please just a thing of contrasts and comparisons.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a George Du Maurier: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Happiness is like time and space-we make and measure it ourselves it is as fancy as big as little…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que felicidade, self, reliance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reliance.

texto: Happiness is like time and space--we make and measure it ourselves; it is a fancy--as big, as little, as you please; just a thing of contrasts and comparisons, like health or strength or beauty or any other good--that wouldn't even be noticed but for sad personal experience of its opposite!--or its greater!

texto: A little work a little play To keep us going - and so good-day!
explicacao: Citação de George Du Maurier sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “A little work a little play To keep us going - and so good-day!” articula reflexao, lighten, little em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e reflexao.

frase_original: A little work a little play To keep us going - and so good-day!

texto: Language is a poor thing. You fill your lungs with wind and shake a little slit in your throat, and make mouths, and that shakes the air; and the air shakes a pair of little drums in my head—a very complicated arrangement, with lots of bones behind—and my brain seizes your meaning in the rough. What a roundabout way, and what a waste of time.

frase_original: Language is a poor thing. You fill your lungs with wind and shake a little slit in your throat, and make mouths, and that shakes the air; and the air shakes a pair of little drums in my head—a very complicated arrangement, with lots of bones behind—and my brain seizes your meaning in the rough. What a roundabout way, and what a waste of time.
